turns.user_message: Someone just handed me another huge pile of tasks and told me to just get it done by tomorrow. I nodded and smiled like I always do, but my stomach completely dropped the moment they walked away. I have been carrying this heavy weight for months now and it feels like it never actually lightens up. I keep pushing through the hours, but my brain just feels completely foggy and heavy. I honestly do not know how much longer I can keep pretending everything is fine when I am running on empty.

They keep adding more to my plate without even checking if I have the bandwidth left to handle it. Every single time they drop off a new request, I feel that familiar knot tighten right in my chest. It has been happening for so long that I barely even react when the notifications start piling up again. I just sit there staring at the screen, completely drained before I even touch a single item. The constant pressure is slowly grinding me down until I feel like a hollow shell of myself.

I watch them casually toss another impossible deadline my way and realize I have absolutely nothing left to give. That same dismissive tone they always use echoes in my head, making my hands feel heavy and useless on the desk. I have been trying to keep up with this endless cycle for ages, but my entire system is finally shutting down. Every single demand feels like a physical weight crushing my ability to think or breathe properly. I just sit in the quiet and let the hours slip by because I cannot force myself to care anymore.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This has been going on for a while.
